TOP with tonic water.
review: A dry, refreshing long drink for those that like their G&Ts gingered.

name: Ginger Cocktail
instructions: MUDDLE ginger in base of shaker
Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
review: This Martini may be served chilled but its flavour is distinctly warming.
history: Discovered in 2003 at Hurricane Bar and Grill, Edinburgh, Scotland.

name: Ginger Mojito
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS.
MUDDLE ginger in base of shaker.
Add mint and lightly MUDDLE (just to bruise).
Add next 3 ingredients and SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into glass filled with crushed ice.
TOP with ginger ale and briefly stir.
garnish: Garnish with mint sprigs bouquet.
review: A ginger spiced variation on the classic Mojito.
history: For more recipes, its origins and history, see the Mojito Cocktail.

name: Gin-Ger Tom
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) water
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS.
MUDDLE ginger in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients.
SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange slice and Luxardo Maraschino Cherry on stick (sail).
review: A Tom Collins with lime and ginger - very refreshing.
history: Adapted from a drink created in 2003 by Jamie Terrell at Lab, London, England.

name: Gingertini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: A delicate cocktail with a warming hint of ginger.
history: Created in 2002 by yours truly in London, England.

name: Giuseppe's Habit
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SQUEEZE the oils from and drop each of the two lemon zests into the shaker.
Add other ingredients and REGAL SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of star anise.
review: An intriguing drink that combines hazelnut, orange, apple, aniseed and peppermint.
history: Created in 2002 by Leon Stokes at Zinc Bar & Grill, Birmingham, England.

name: Give me a Dime
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
Crumble a piece of Cadbury's Flake over the cocktail.
garnish: Prepare garnish of Cadbury's Flake.
review: Creamy, sweet and tasty.

name: Gloom Chaser (Savoy)
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: A sunny coloured drink for happy souls. And sweet orange and pomegranate soured with lemon would make anyone happy.
history: Adapted from a recipe in Harry Craddock's 1930 The Savoy Cocktail Book.

name: Georgetown Punch
instructions: Select and pre-chill a HIGHBALL (MAX 10OZ/300ML) GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of pineapple wedge.
review: A Tiki-style, fruity rum punch.
history: Adapted from a drink discovered in 2005 at Degrees, Washington DC, USA.

name: Apple Pie Cocktail
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of apple wedge.
review: There's a good hit of cinnamon in this apple pie.
history: Created in 2000 by Alexia Pau Barrera at Sand Bar, Clapham, England.

name: Golden Bird
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange 'beak' on rim.
review: Fruity and sweet - an after dinner cocktail.

name: Golden Dawn
instructions: SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass
Carefully POUR grenadine into centre of drink so that it sinks to create a sunrise effect.
review: Although it spoils the sunrise effect, this drink is less tart if the syrup lying on the bottom is stirred into the drink (or, better, included when shaking).
history: Created in September 1930 by Tom Buttery at the Berkeley Hotel, London and first published in William J. "Billy" Tarling's 1937 Café Royal Bar Book. There are now many versions of this classic cocktail (David Embury's The Fine Art of Mixing Drinks lists three), the above recipe is my favourite.

name: Golden Fizz No.2
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS.
STIR honey with gin in base of shaker until honey is dissolved.
Add next 3 ingredients and SHAKE with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass whilst also pouring lemonade.
garnish: Garnish with orange slice and mint sprigs bouquet.
review: More cloudy white than golden but a pleasant, refreshing long drink all the same.
history: Adapted from a recipe created in the late 1990s by Wayne Collins, London, England.

name: Golden Girl
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Dust with grated orange zest.
review: This appropriately named velvety drink is a refined dessert in a glass.
history: Adapted from a recipe created by Dale DeGroff in New York City, USA. I've slightly increased the proportions of rum and port from Dale's original recipe.

name: Golf Cocktail
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
STIR all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: A 'wet' Martini with bitters.

name: Cadillac Margarita (AKA Grand Margarita)
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lime wedge and half salt rim (optional).
review: A balanced (far from sweet) and flavoursome Margarita with the rich cognac and orange notes of the liqueur adding to this drink's depth of flavour.
history: The Cadillac Margarita, with Grand Marnier in place of triple sec liqueur, became well known in the 1980s due to being marketed by the Southern California-based El Torito Grill chain of restaurants.

name: Grand Mimosa
instructions: Select and pre-chill a GOBLET GLASS.
POUR half the sparkling wine into ice-filled glass.
ADD orange juice and orange liqueur.
TOP with the rest of the sparkling wine.
garnish: Garnish with orange slice wheel.
review: As the name suggests, the orange of Grand Marnier heavily influences this drink. Basically a Mimosa or a Buck's Fizz (orange juice and champagne) with added oomph.
history:
The Mimosa was created in 1925 at the Ritz Hotel in Paris, France. The Grand Mimosa benefits from a splash of Grand Marnier liqueur.
The differences between a Buck's Fizz and Mimosa and their history

name: Granny's
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of apple wedge.
review: Apple, rum and cinnamon were made for each other.

name: Grape Delight
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients and SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with skewered grapes.
review: This rust coloured cocktail is fruity and delicate.

name: Grape Effect
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients.
SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of red and white grapes.
review: Delicately flavoured and heavily laced with rum.

name: Grape Martini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients.
SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with grapes.
review: Simple but remarkably tasty.
history: Created in 2004 by yours truly, London, England.

name: Apple Spritz
instructions: Select a FLUTE GLASS.
POUR first 2 ingredients into chilled glass.
TOP with prosecco.
garnish: Garnish with pear wedge on rim.
review: Sweet, fruity fizz.
history:
Discovered in 2003 at Paramount Hotel in New York City, USA.
Spritz history and the Spritz family of cocktails

name: Grapefruit Daiquiri No.1
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with maraschino cherry.
review: The flavours of rum and grapefruit combine harmoniously - clean and fresh.

name: Grapparita
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker.
DRY SHAKE (without ice).
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lemon zest twist (expressed and discarded) and lime wedge on rim .
review: Grappa replaces tequila and lemon liqueur triple sec in this Italian twist in the classic Margarita.
history: Adapted from a drink discovered in 2005 at Alfredo's of Rome, New York City, USA. The original called for a sour mix.

name: Grapple Martini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients.
SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with grapes.
review: A rounded, fruity Martini-style drink.
history: Adapted from a recipe created in 2003 by Chris Setchell at Las Iguanas, UK.

name: Grassy Finnish
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE lemongrass in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients and SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with lemongrass.
review: Like Finland, this drink is clean, green, wooded and safe, but deep down there's plenty of spice.
history: Created in 2003 by Gerard McCurry at Revolution, UK.

name: Green Apple and Cucumber Martini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients.
SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of cucumber slices.
review: Archetypal English flavours. Clean, green and refreshing.
history: Adapted from a recipe discovered in 2003 at Oxo Tower Bar, London, England.

name: Green Fairy
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker.
DRY SHAKE (without ice).
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS lemon zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lemon zest twist.
review: A well-balanced Absinthe Sour served straight-up.
history: Created in the 1990s by Dick Bradsell, London, England.

name: Green Hornet
instructions: Select and pre-chill a SHOT GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
review: A surprisingly palatable and balanced shot.

name: Green Tea Cocktail #1
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of banana chunk.
review: It's green and, although it doesn't actually contain any tea, has something of the flavour of alcoholic peppermint tea.

name: Greyhound
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Garnish with grapefruit slice wheel.
review: Classically a Greyhound is merely vodka and grapefruit juice served tall over ice in a Collins glass, but it's better served shorter with a couple of flavour-enhancing embellishments (added by yours truly).
history: A lesser known relative to the Salty Dog. The earliest recipe I have found is in Stanley M. Jones 1977 Jones' Complete Barguide.

name: Gulf Coast Sex on the Beach
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
review: Golden tan in colour and tropical in flavour, complete with frothy top.
history: Created in 1997 by Roberto Canino and Wayne Collins at Navajo Joe, London, England.

name: Gusto
instructions: MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker
Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
review: A pleasing long drink flavoured with apple, grape and tequila.
history: Created in 2003 by Thomas Gillgren at The Kingly Club, London, England.

name: Gypsy No. 2
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
MUDDLE rosemary and raisins with gin in base of shaker.
Add other ingredients and SHAKE with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with rosemary sprig.
review: Jason's original recipe called for raisin infused gin and I'd recommend you make this drink that way if time permits.
history: Adapted from a recipe created by Jason Fendick in 2002 for Steam, London, England.

name: Juicy Gypsy
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: Fresh and juicy, with herbal complexity.
history: A riff on the classic Gypsy Queen by yours truly.

name: Hakkatini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: Balanced bittersweet orange and apple.
history: Adapted from a drink discovered in 2003 at Hakkasan, London, England.

name: Hard Lemonade
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) water
instructions: SHAKE first three ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass
TOP with soda and serve with straws.
review: Refreshing lemonade with a kick. Great for a hot afternoon.
history: Discovered in 2004 at Spring Street Natural Restaurant, New York City, USA.

name: Harvard Cooler
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COLLINS GLASS.
SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
TOP with soda.
garnish: Garnish with lime wedge.
review: Refreshing and far from too sweet. Lime and sugar enhance the appley spirit.

name: Havana Cobbler
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into glass filled with crushed ice.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lime twist or wedge.
review: An unusual, spiced, Daiquiri-like drink.

name: Hawaiian Cocktail
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of pineapple wedge and Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.
review: Sweet, tangy and fruity.
history: Adapted from a cocktail we discovered in 2004 in Las Vegas, USA.

name: Hawaiian Cosmopolitan
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of pineapple wedge.
review: Fresh, tangy and distinctly tropical.
history:
Created in 2002 by Wayne Collins, UK.

For more recipes, origins and history behind the Cosmopolitan cocktail, see our Cosmopolitan page.

name: Hawaiian Martini
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with pineapple wedge and maraschino cherry.
review: An aptly named fruity twist on the classic Martini.
history: Adapted from a drink discovered in 2005 at the Four Seasons Hotel in Milan, Italy.

name: Hazelnut Alexander
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker.
DRY SHAKE (without ice).
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Dust with cocoa powder.
review: A nutty riff on the Brandy Alexander. The use of bitters is inspired.
history: Created in 2005 by James Mellnor at Maze, London, England.

name: Hemingway
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
review: Delicately sweetened lime and fizz with faint notes of aniseed from the absinthe.
history: A lime influenced riff on Ernest Hemingway's classic Death in the Afternoon. Another version of this cocktail, apparently created at Cantineros' Club, the famous Cuban bar school, calls for a shot of Pernod anis to be poured into a chilled glass and then topped with champagne.

name: The Hive
instructions: STIR honey with vodka until honey dissolves
Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
review: Sour grapefruit balanced by sweet honey.
history: Discovered in 2004 at Circus, London, England.

name: Hoa Sua
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with pineapple wedge on rim.
review: Light and easy drinking - the rum flavour shines through.
history: Created in 2005 by yours truly for Hoa Sua catering school in Vietnam using locally available products.

name: Honey Apple Cocktail
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS lemon zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lemon zest twist.
review: A classically Polish blend of flavours.

name: Honey Berry Sour
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of lemon wedge.
review: More sweet than sour but berry nice.
history: Created by Tim Hallilaj, London, England.

name: Honey Limeaid
instructions: STIR (7 spoons of) honey with lime juice in base of shaker until honey dissolves
SHAKE with ice and strain into ice-filled glass
TOP with soda.
review: A refreshing Mexican variation on Real Lemonade.
history: Discovered in 2005 at Hotel Quinta Real in Guadalajara, Mexico.

name: Honey Vodka Sour
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
STRAIN into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Garnish with lime wedge.
review: A vodka sour with true honey character.

name: Honey Wall
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
STIR all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS flamed orange zest twist over cocktail, then use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: Rum-laced, rich and chocolaty.
history: Adapted from a drink created in 2002 by Dick Bradsell at Downstairs at Alfred's in London, England.

name: The Honeysuckle Orchard
instructions: STIR honey with vodka in base of shaker until honey dissolves
Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
review: A back-to-nature Polish Martini - all the better for it.
history: Discovered in 2005 at The Stanton Social, New York City, USA.

name: Honolulu
instructions: Select and pre-chill an OLD-FASHIONED GLASS.
DRY BLEND (without ice) first 5 ingredients on slow setting and then gradually add Xanthan gum while continuing to blend.
Add 7oz scoop of crushed ice and BLEND some more.
POUR into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with pineapple wedge on rim skewered with maraschino cherry.
review: A fruity frozen rum-laced sour. Perfect for a hot afternoon in Honolulu.
history: Adapted from a recipe in Victor Bergeron's 1972 Trader Vic's Bartender's Guide (revised edition) with his TV logo alongside the recipe denoting that this is one of Trader Vic's own creations, confirmed by his writing, "This is one of the first drinks that we ever originated."

name: Hoopla
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: Not far removed from a Sidecar.
history: A riff on the Hoop La! Cocktail recipe in Harry Craddock's 1930 The Savoy Cocktail Book. The same book also features two other cocktails with the exact same recipe: the Frank Sullivan Cocktail and Odd McIntyre Cocktail.

name: Hop Toad #2
instructions: Select and pre-chill a COUPE GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Garnish with apricot wedge or half.
review: Alcoholic apricot jam with a lovely twang of aged rum.

name: Hot Buttered Rum Cocktail
instructions: Select and pre-heat a TODDY GLASS.
Place bar spoon loaded with honey in warmed glass.
Add other ingredients.
STIR until honey and butter are dissolved.
garnish: Garnish with cinnamon stick and lemon slice (studded with cloves).
review:
In his The Fine Art of Mixing Drinks, David Embury says, "The Hot Spiced Rum is bad enough, but the lump of butter is the final insult. It blends with the hot rum just about as satisfactorily as warm olive oil blends with champagne!" 

It's rare for us to question Embury but we rather like this slightly oily, warming, spicy toddy. This cocktail is even better when you use heated dry cider instead of boiling water.

name: Hot Grog
instructions: Place bar spoon loaded with honey in warmed glass
Add other ingredients and STIR until honey dissolves.
review: Warming, honeyed, pungent rum with a hint of lime.

name: Hot Red Blooded Frenchman
instructions: Place bar spoon in warmed glass
Add other ingredients and STIR.
review: Warm, fruity red wine - great on a cold night.

name: Hot Tub
instructions: SHAKE first three ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass
TOP with prosecco.
review: Basically, a French Martini with bubbles.
history: Adapted from a cocktail discovered in 2004 at Teatro in Boston, USA.

name: I B Damm'd
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
SHAKE all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
garnish: Prepare garnish of peach wedge on rim.
review: Subtle combination of fruit and floral flavours.
history: Discovered in 2003 at Oxo Tower Bar, London, England.

name: Ice Maiden
instructions: Select and pre-chill a MARTINI GLASS.
STIR all ingredients with ice.
FINE STRAIN into chilled glass.
EXPRESS orange zest twist over the cocktail and use as garnish.
garnish: Prepare garnish of orange zest twist.
review: A subtle Martini with the honeyed flavours of icewine melding with botanicals in the gin and balanced by the acidity of the white wine.
history: Created in 2004 by yours truly, London, England.
